skill.name: javascript-testing-patterns
skill.description: Implement comprehensive testing strategies using Jest, Vitest, and Testing Library for unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end testing with mocking, fixtures, and test-driven development.
skill.summary: Comprehensive testing patterns and best practices for JavaScript/TypeScript applications using modern frameworks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 145 static findings are false positives. The detected patterns exist only in code examples within markdown documentation files, not in executable code. The skill provides educational content about testing patterns with code samples demonstrating legitimate testing practices including mock setup, API testing, email service testing, and database integration testing. No executable code, no security risks.

content.value_statement: Writing tests is hard. This skill provides proven patterns and examples for unit, integration, and end-to-end testing using Jest, Vitest, and Testing Library so you can build reliable test suites quickly.
actual_capabilities: Generate unit tests for functions, classes, and async code using Jest or Vitest
Create integration tests for APIs and databases with proper setup and teardown
Write frontend component tests using React Testing Library and Vue Test Utils
Implement mocking patterns for external dependencies, APIs, and modules
Set up test fixtures and factories for consistent test data generation
Configure test coverage reporting and CI/CD integration
limitations: Only supports JavaScript/TypeScript testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Testing Library)
Cannot execute tests directly - only generates test code and configuration
Does not provide test runner infrastructure or CI/CD pipeline setup beyond configuration examples
Limited to documented patterns - may not cover all edge cases or custom testing scenarios

use_cases.title: New Project Test Setup
use_cases.description: Developers starting a new JavaScript/TypeScript project who need to establish test infrastructure from scratch with proper framework configuration, directory structure, and CI/CD integration.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developer starting a new project

use_cases.title: Add Tests to Existing Codebase
use_cases.description: Teams maintaining legacy or untested applications who need to gradually introduce testing for existing functions, APIs, and components without breaking existing functionality.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineer working on legacy code

use_cases.title: Improve Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Developers seeking to increase code coverage and improve test quality by learning advanced mocking patterns, fixture factories, and integration testing strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Unit Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Write unit tests for a function that [describe what function does]. Include tests for success cases and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Component Test with Testing Library
prompt_templates.prompt: Create React Testing Library tests for a component that [describe component behavior]. Test user interactions, form submissions, and error states.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing React or Vue frontend components

prompt_templates.title: API Integration Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Write integration tests for a REST API endpoint that [describe endpoint functionality]. Include database setup, authentication, and cleanup using supertest.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing backend API endpoints with database

prompt_templates.title: Mock External Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create tests for [service/class] that mock external dependencies including [list dependencies]. Use dependency injection and vi.mock patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing code with external API or service calls

output_examples.input: Write unit tests for an add function that takes two numbers and returns their sum
Create a test for a user form component that validates email and submits data
output: import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
it('should show validation error for invalid email', () => {
it('should call onSubmit with form data', () => {

best_practices: Follow the Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) pattern in every test for clear structure and readability
Test behavior and user interactions rather than implementation details to make tests resilient to refactoring
Use beforeEach and afterEach hooks for setup and teardown to keep tests isolated and prevent pollution
anti_patterns: Testing implementation details like internal function calls or component state instead of observable behavior
Writing brittle tests that break when code is refactored but behavior remains unchanged
Creating overly complex test setups with too many mocks that make tests hard to understand and maintain

faq.question: Should I use Jest or Vitest?
faq.answer: Vitest is faster and works better with Vite projects. Jest is more mature and has wider adoption. Choose Vitest if you use Vite, otherwise Jest is a safe choice.

faq.question: How do I test code that uses external APIs?
faq.answer: Mock the external API calls using vi.mock() or dependency injection. This keeps tests fast, reliable, and independent of external services.

faq.question: What test coverage should I aim for?
faq.answer: Aim for 80% coverage as a general target. Focus on covering critical business logic and error paths rather than chasing 100% coverage.

faq.question: Should I test private methods?
faq.answer: No. Test public behavior only. If private methods need testing, consider refactoring them into separate, testable units.

faq.question: How do I handle database tests?
faq.answer: Use a separate test database and clean data between tests using beforeEach. Consider using transactions for faster rollback instead of truncating tables.

faq.question: What is the difference between unit and integration tests?
faq.answer: Unit tests isolate individual functions or classes. Integration tests verify multiple components work together, including databases and APIs.
